The plan includes retrieve the unnormalized logits vector for the next token from the language model.
The plan includes optionally divide logits by the temperature τ (if τ != 1.0) to control randomness.
The plan includes convert the (temperature-scaled) logits to probabilities using softmax.
The plan includes identify the maximum token probability p_max = max(probs).
The plan includes compute the dynamic truncation threshold scaled_min_p = p_base * p_max.
The plan includes create a boolean mask marking tokens whose probability is less than scaled_min_p as candidates for removal.
The plan includes sort token indices by original logit scores in descending order.
The plan includes modify the removal mask to keep at least min_tokens_to_keep tokens (default 1) by setting the top min_tokens_to_keep positions in the sorted order to not be removed.
The plan includes apply the final removal mask to the logits, setting the values of removed tokens to -inf (or a very low filter value).
The plan includes use the filtered logits for sampling (e.g., multinomial sampling) to select the next token.
The plan includes the setting 'min-p (p_base=0.05)': Base threshold 0.05 – higher diversity setting, recommended range for creative tasks.
The plan includes the setting 'min-p (p_base=0.1)': Base threshold 0.1 – balanced setting between coherence and diversity.
The plan includes the setting 'min-p (p_base=0.2)': Base threshold 0.2 – lower diversity, used in human evaluation for conservative sampling.
The plan includes the use of the GPQA Main dataset.
The plan includes the use of the GSM8K dataset.
The plan includes the use of the AlpacaEval Creative Writing dataset.
The plan includes the use of the Mistral 7B model.
The plan includes the use of the Mistral Large (123B) model.
The plan includes the use of the Llama 3 (1B–70B) model.
The plan includes the use of the Llama 3.2 70B model.
The plan includes the use of the Llama 3 70B model.
The plan includes the Temperature Only baseline for comparison.
The plan includes the Top-p baseline for comparison.
The plan includes the Top-k baseline for comparison.
The plan includes the η Sampling baseline for comparison.
The plan includes the ε Sampling baseline for comparison.
The plan includes the Mirostat baseline for comparison.
The plan includes the Standard Sampling (baseline for creative writing comparison) baseline for comparison.

description: The experiment under the 'min-p (p_base=0.05)' setting has been successfully executed and produced its expected output.
The experiment under the 'min-p (p_base=0.1)' setting has been successfully executed and produced its expected output.
The experiment under the 'min-p (p_base=0.2)' setting has been successfully executed and produced its expected output.

The execution outcomes match the results in Table 2: Min-p sampling outperforms top-p, top-k, temperature-only, and entropy-based methods on GPQA Main and GSM8K CoT benchmarks, particularly at higher temperatures (τ ≥ 1.5), maintaining accuracy while baselines degrade sharply.

The execution outcomes match the results in Table 3a: Min-p's advantages persist with larger models; on Mistral Large (123B), min-p maintains high accuracy at temperatures up to τ=4.0 while top-p and temperature-only drop to near zero.

The execution outcomes match the results in Table 3b: Min-p achieves higher win rates than top-p, mirostat, and ε-sampling on the AlpacaEval Creative Writing benchmark, demonstrating superior creative writing quality.

The execution outcomes match the results in Figure 2: Min-p sampling yields a better accuracy–diversity trade-off than top-p on GSM8K CoT Self-Consistency, with configurations closer to the Pareto frontier and covering a wider range of hyperparameter sensitivity.

The execution outcomes match the results in Table 4: Human evaluators rate min-p outputs higher than top-p in both quality and diversity, especially at high temperatures, confirming real-world preference for min-p in creative writing.

The execution outcomes match the results in Table 7: Ablation on AlpacaEval Creative Writing shows min-p consistently outperforms top-p across different temperature and parameter configurations, with the highest winrate at τ=1.5 and p_base=0.1.

The execution outcomes match the results in Table 9: Min-p's benefits transfer across model families: on Llama 3.1 70B, min-p matches or exceeds top-p on GPQA Main and GSM8K CoT, particularly at elevated temperatures.

The execution outcomes match the results in Table 12: Combining min-p with other truncation methods (e.g., top-p + min-p) leads to double normalization issues and suboptimal performance, supporting the recommendation to use min-p as the sole truncation method.
The execution outcomes match the results in Table 8: Llama 3 models with min-p sampling outperform top-p on GPQA and GSM8K CoT benchmarks, especially at high temperatures (>1.0).

The execution outcomes match the specific claim (based on Table 2): Min-p maintains substantially higher accuracy than top-p, temperature-only, and top-k on GPQA Main and GSM8K CoT at τ = 1.5, 2.0, and 3.0.
The execution outcomes match the specific claim (based on Figure 2): Min-p achieves a superior accuracy–diversity Pareto frontier compared to top-p on GSM8K CoT Self-Consistency.

The execution outcomes match the specific claim (based on Table 4): In human evaluation, min-p scores higher than top-p on both quality and diversity across all temperature and diversity settings, with statistically significant differences at τ=3.0.

The execution outcomes match the specific claim (based on Table 3a): Min-p scales to large models (123B parameters), retaining GPQA Main accuracy above 22% at τ=3.0 while top-p and temperature-only fall below 3%.
The execution outcomes match the specific claim (based on Table 3b): Min-p outperforms top-p on the AlpacaEval Creative Writing benchmark, achieving higher win rates at τ=1.0 and τ=1.5.

The execution outcomes match the specific claim (based on Table 2): Min-p is computationally efficient compared to entropy-based methods (η/ε sampling) whose runtime grows exponentially with temperature and which fail entirely at τ ≥ 2.0.

The execution outcomes match the specific claim (based on Table 1): Min-p dynamically adjusts its truncation threshold: in high-certainty contexts it preserves only top relevant tokens, while in low-certainty contexts it permits a diverse set, as illustrated by token probability comparisons in case studies.
